headline: Honda Recalls 2025-2026 CRF250R and CRF450R Off-Road Motorcycles Due to Handlebar Clamp Safety Risk
description: American Honda recalls about 12,600 2025-2026 CRF250R and CRF450R off-road motorcycles due to handlebar clamp defect posing crash hazard risk.
keywords: Model Year 2025-2026 CRF250R/RX/RWE and CRF450R/RX/RWE Off-Road Motorcycles
The handlebar's clamp can unexpectedly cause the handlebar to rotate, posing a risk of serious injury or death due to a crash hazard.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: American Honda is recalling approximately 12,600 model year 2025-2026 CRF250R/RX/RWE and CRF450R/RX/RWE off-road motorcycles because a defective handlebar clamp can cause the handlebar to rotate unexpectedly, increasing the risk of a crash. Consumers should stop using these motorcycles immediately and contact an authorized Honda Powersports dealer for a free inspection and repair or replacement of the handlebar clamp.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves model year 2025-2026 Honda off-road motorcycles with model numbers CRF250R, CRF250RX, CRF250RWE, CRF450R, CRF450RX, and CRF450RWE. These motorcycles feature a red chassis and a white Honda logo on both sides of the rear. The model number is displayed in white letters on the front of the bike. The vehicle identification number (VIN) is located on the left side of the steering head.

About 12,600 units were sold nationwide through authorized Honda Powersports dealers from June 2024 through March 2026, with an additional six units sold in Canada. The motorcycles retailed for prices ranging from $8,900 to $13,200. The recall was issued due to a defect in the handlebar clamp that can cause the handlebar to rotate unexpectedly, creating a crash hazard.

American Honda has received one report of a crash related to this defect but no injuries have been reported. Consumers can verify if their motorcycle is included in the recall by checking the VIN at Honda’s official recall website.

products.Name: Model Year 2025-2026 CRF250R/RX/RWE and CRF450R/RX/RWE Off-Road Motorcycles
products.NumberOfUnits: About 12,600 (In addition, about six were sold in Canada)

hazards: The handlebar's clamp can unexpectedly cause the handlebar to rotate, posing a risk of serious injury or death due to a crash hazard.
remedies: Consumers should stop using the recalled off-road motorcycles immediately and contact an authorized Honda Powersports dealer to schedule an appointment for a free inspection and if necessary, a replacement of the handlebar's clamp at the dealer's location.
injuries: The firm has received one report of a crash. No injuries have been reported.
retailers: Authorized Honda Powersports dealers nationwide from June 2024 through March 2026 for between $8,900 and $13,200.
importers: American Honda Motor Co. Inc, of Torrance, California

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify if your motorcycle is part of this recall, check the following details:

  • Model year: 2025 or 2026
  • Model number: CRF250R, CRF250RX, CRF250RWE, CRF450R, CRF450RX, or CRF450RWE. The model number is printed in white letters on the front of the motorcycle.
  • Appearance: The motorcycle has a red chassis and a white Honda logo on both sides of the rear.
  • VIN location: The vehicle identification number (VIN) is located on the left side of the steering head.

You can confirm if your motorcycle is included in the recall by entering the VIN at Honda’s recall website: https://powersports.honda.com/recalls.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
If you own a recalled Honda off-road motorcycle, stop using it immediately to avoid the risk of a crash. Contact an authorized Honda Powersports dealer to schedule a free inspection. If the handlebar clamp is found to be defective, the dealer will repair or replace the clamp at no cost to you.

For more information or assistance, you can call American Honda Motor toll-free at 866-784-1870, Monday through Friday from 8:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. PT, or visit https://powersports.honda.com and click on "Recalls" at the top of the page.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text: The handlebar clamp on the recalled Honda off-road motorcycles may fail to hold the handlebar securely. If the clamp loosens or allows the handlebar to rotate unexpectedly while riding, the rider could lose control of the motorcycle. This loss of control increases the risk of a crash, which could result in serious injury or death.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text: This recall affects owners of 2025-2026 Honda CRF250R, CRF250RX, CRF250RWE, CRF450R, CRF450RX, and CRF450RWE off-road motorcycles. These motorcycles were sold through authorized Honda Powersports dealers nationwide and a small number in Canada. If you own one of these models, your motorcycle may be subject to this recall.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: American Honda Motor toll-free at 866-784-1870 from 8:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, or online at https://powersports.honda.com and click on "Recalls" at the top of the page for more information or at https://powersports.honda.com/recalls.

faq.question: Which Honda off-road motorcycle models are included in this recall?
faq.answer: The recall includes model year 2025-2026 Honda CRF250R, CRF250RX, CRF250RWE, CRF450R, CRF450RX, and CRF450RWE off-road motorcycles.

faq.question: What is the safety hazard associated with this recall?
faq.answer: The handlebar clamp can unexpectedly allow the handlebar to rotate, increasing the risk of a crash that could cause serious injury or death.

faq.question: How can I check if my motorcycle is affected by this recall?
faq.answer: You can check your motorcycle’s vehicle identification number (VIN), located on the left side of the steering head, at Honda’s recall website: https://powersports.honda.com/recalls.

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: Recalls Atlas has summarized this recall based on the official U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) recall notice. The original recall details and updates are maintained by the CPSC and can be accessed through their website.
authorityLinks: Read the official CPSC recall notice on the CPSC website.

eeatMeta.factualBasis: Official U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission recall record.
eeatMeta.limitations: This page is not independent product testing, medical advice, legal advice, or a human injury investigation.

headline: Build-A-Bear Workshop Recalls Heartwarming Hugs Bears Over Zipper Choking Risk
description: Build-A-Bear recalls Heartwarming Hugs Bears due to detachable zipper slider posing choking hazard to children. About 36,000 units sold in the US and Canada.
keywords: The zipper slider on the side pouch can detach, posing a risk of serious injury or death from choking hazard to children.

content.subtitle: Quick Answer
content.text: The Heartwarming Hugs weighted plush bear by Build-A-Bear Workshop is recalled because the zipper slider on its side pouch can detach, creating a choking hazard for children. Consumers should stop using the bear immediately and return it to Build-A-Bear for a refund or gift card.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
This recall involves the Heartwarming Hugs weighted plush bear with model number 034464 sold by Build-A-Bear Workshop. The bear features a side pouch containing a heart-shaped bag filled with 2.5 pounds of ceramic beads that can be heated or cooled for comfort. The pouch is secured by a zipper, which is the source of the hazard.

About 36,000 units were sold in the United States, with an additional 520 sold in Canada, from January 2026 through March 2026. These bears were available at Build-A-Bear Workshop stores and online at www.buildabear.com for approximately $48.

There has been one report from the United Kingdom of the zipper slider detaching during use, but no injuries or incidents have been reported in the United States.

products.Name: Build-A-Bear Workshop Heartwarming Hugs Bears
products.NumberOfUnits: About 36,000 (In addition 520 were sold in Canada)

hazards: The zipper slider on the side pouch can detach, posing a risk of serious injury or death from choking hazard to children.
remedies: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled Heartwarming Hugs Bear and return it to a local Build-A-Bear Workshop store to receive a refund in the form of original payment or a gift card for the original purchase price redeemable toward any available Make-Your-Own furry friend and any other available merchandise. If a consumer is unable to return their Heartwarming Hugs Bear to a local store, they can visit www.buildabear.com/recalls for more information or call Build-A-Bear to receive a free return shipping label to return the recalled bear and to receive a refund check in the form of original payment or in the form of a digital gift card to use on the firm's website.
injuries: There has been one consumer report in the United Kingdom of the zipper slider detaching while in use, and no reports in the United States. No injuries have been reported.
retailers: Build-A-Bear Workshop stores and online at www.buildabear.com from January 2026 through March 2026 for about $48.
importers: Build-A-Bear Workshop, Inc., of St Louis, Missouri

content.subtitle: How to Identify the Recalled Product
content.text:
To identify the recalled Heartwarming Hugs bear, look for the following details:

  • Product: Heartwarming Hugs weighted plush bear
  • Model number: 034464 (found on the label sewn into the back side of one bear leg)
  • Features a side pouch with a zipper containing a heart-shaped bag filled with ceramic beads
  • Sold at Build-A-Bear Workshop stores and online from January 2026 through March 2026

Images of the bear show the side pouch with the zipper and the heart-shaped bead bag inside.

content.subtitle: What Consumers Should Do
content.text:
If you own a Heartwarming Hugs bear, immediately stop using it and keep it away from children. Return the bear to any local Build-A-Bear Workshop store to receive a refund in the original form of payment or a gift card redeemable toward any available Make-Your-Own furry friend or other merchandise.

If you cannot return the bear to a store, visit www.buildabear.com/recalls or call Build-A-Bear at 844-541-0144 to request a free return shipping label. After returning the bear by mail, you will receive a refund check or a digital gift card for the original purchase price.

content.subtitle: Risk Overview
content.text:
The zipper slider on the side pouch of the Heartwarming Hugs bear can detach unexpectedly. If a child puts the detached zipper slider in their mouth, it poses a serious choking hazard. This risk is especially concerning for young children who may not recognize the danger of small parts.

The bear is intended for children aged 3 years and older and includes a cautionary statement advising adult supervision due to the heated or cooled ceramic bead pouch. However, the zipper failure creates an unforeseen risk that could lead to serious injury or death if choking occurs.

content.subtitle: Who May Be Affected
content.text:
This recall affects consumers who purchased the Heartwarming Hugs weighted plush bear, model number 034464, from Build-A-Bear Workshop stores or online between January and March 2026. The product is intended for children aged 3 years and older.

If you have this bear in your home, especially if it is used by a child, you should take immediate action to prevent any risk.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Build-A-Bear at 844-541-0144 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT Monday through Saturday and 7 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT on Sunday or by email at ProductHotline@buildabear.com, or online at https://www.buildabear.com/recalls or https://www.buildabear.com and click on "Recall" at the bottom of the page for more information.

faq.question: What is the hazard associated with the Heartwarming Hugs bear?
faq.answer: The zipper slider on the bear’s side pouch can detach, posing a choking hazard to children if swallowed.

faq.question: How can I tell if my Heartwarming Hugs bear is part of the recall?
faq.answer: Check the label sewn into the back side of one bear leg for model number 034464 and verify the bear has a side pouch with a zipper containing a heart-shaped ceramic bead bag.

faq.question: What should I do if I have a recalled Heartwarming Hugs bear?
faq.answer: Stop using the bear immediately and return it to a Build-A-Bear Workshop store for a refund or gift card. If you cannot visit a store, contact Build-A-Bear for a free return shipping label and refund options.

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: Recalls Atlas has summarized this recall based on the official U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) recall notice. The original recall data and detailed information are preserved and accessible through the CPSC website.
authorityLinks: Read the official CPSC recall notice on the CPSC website.

Hazards.Name: The zipper slider on the side pouch can detach, posing a risk of serious injury or death from choking hazard to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled Heartwarming Hugs Bear and return it to a local Build-A-Bear Workshop store to receive a refund in the form of original payment or a gift card for the original purchase price redeemable toward any available Make-Your-Own furry friend and any other available merchandise. If a consumer is unable to return their Heartwarming Hugs Bear to a local store, they can visit www.buildabear.com/recalls for more information or call Build-A-Bear to receive a free return shipping label to return the recalled bear and to receive a refund check in the form of original payment or in the form of a digital gift card to use on the firm's website.
